metadata.summary: Three-layer architecture: components/ui/ (vendored), components/<feature>/ (compositions), lib/ (utilities). CI guards prevent primitive mutation and cross-feature imports.

content.title: shadcn/ui Architecture
content.summary: shadcn/ui components are vendored (copied, not installed as packages) into components/ui/. The architecture enforces three strict layers: ui/ primitives are mutated only via npx shadcn add; components/<feature>/ composes from ui/; lib/ holds pure utilities. All variants use cva(). Theme via CSS variables only — no hex literals in feature components.

why.p: Without layer guards, agents edit components/ui/button.tsx directly to "fix one thing", breaking future shadcn diff upgrades. CI import rules (dependency-cruiser or eslint-plugin-boundaries) make the contract enforceable rather than social.

list.item: Mid-to-large React + Tailwind app using shadcn/ui that needs directory ownership rules.
Multiple agents or teams contributing components — prevents ui/ becoming a junk drawer.
Migrating from a closed UI library into a layered primitives → composite → feature structure.
Monorepo where shadcn primitives live in a shared packages/ui/.
App with fewer than 20 components — flat components/ is simpler.
Stack is not React + Tailwind; these rules assume both.
Project uses MUI/Mantine; different composition patterns apply.
Pre-shadcn-init phase; set up shadcn-ui methodology first, then add architecture rules.

rule.statement: Enforce exactly three layers: components/ui/ (vendored primitives), components/<feature>/ (compositions), lib/ (pure utilities). Resist adding a fourth.
rule.rationale: More layers create ambiguous ownership; agents dump files at the wrong level and import rules become unenforceable.

example.title: Three-layer directory structure
example.code: src/ ├── components/ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn vendored — mutate only via npx shadcn add │ ├── billing/ # feature: CompanyBillingCard, etc. │ └── layout/ # page chrome: Header, Sidebar ├── lib/ │ └── utils.ts # cn() and pure helpers └── styles/ └── globals.css # CSS variables + @tailwind directives

section.name: cn-helper

rule.statement: Always use cn() for className composition. Ban inline className string concatenation longer than 2 utilities.
rule.rationale: tailwind-merge deduplicates conflicting classes (e.g., two px- values); without it, order-dependent class conflicts produce invisible bugs.

section.reference: Canonical cn() helper using clsx + tailwind-merge

rule.statement: Theme via CSS variables (--background, --primary, --ring, etc.) in globals.css. Feature components consume bg-background, text-primary, etc. No hex literals in feature components.
rule.rationale: Hex literals defeat design token contracts; changing the brand color requires a grep rather than a variable update.

rule.statement: For multi-tenant apps, scope variables under [data-theme="tenant-x"] on the html element. Design this up front — adding scoping after the fact requires touching every component.
rule.rationale: CSS variable scoping is the only approach that avoids React Context overhead and works across server and client components.

rule.statement: Use cva() for all variants in feature components. Banned: long inline class strings, dynamic class interpolation, ternary className expressions with more than 2 branches.
rule.rationale: cva produces a typed variant map and integrates with tailwind-merge; inline ternaries grow to 10+ branches within weeks and become unreadable.

example.title: cva() variant management

rule.statement: Add "use client" to every primitive that uses Radix portals, state, or effects. Default to Server Component for compositions unless they import a client primitive.
rule.rationale: Missing "use client" on Radix portal primitives works in dev (no SSR) and silently fails on production server build.

rule.statement: In monorepos, always pass --cwd to the shadcn CLI to target the correct package. One components.json per app or package.
rule.rationale: shadcn CLI uses components.json to locate the target directory; without --cwd it writes to the repo root, not the package.

example.title: Monorepo CLI usage
example.code: npx shadcn@latest add button --cwd packages/ui npx shadcn@latest diff --cwd packages/ui # show upgrade diff before accepting

rule.statement: Before upgrading primitives, run npx shadcn diff to review changes. Visual regression (Chromatic) must pass after any primitive update.
rule.rationale: Shadcn primitives change between releases; accepting updates without review can silently alter component API or accessibility behavior.

antipattern.description: Editing components/ui/button.tsx directly to "fix" a behavior instead of composing a feature component on top.
antipattern.reason: Modifying vendored primitives breaks shadcn diff upgrades and makes the ownership contract meaningless. Install CODEOWNERS or a CI guard to prevent writes to components/ui/.

antipattern.description: Hardcoding color hex values (e.g., text-[#3b82f6]) in feature components.
antipattern.reason: Hard-coded colors bypass the CSS variable theme; brand changes require a grep across every feature instead of a single globals.css update.

antipattern.description: Omitting "use client" on Radix-based primitives (Dialog, Popover, DropdownMenu) in Next.js App Router.
antipattern.reason: Works in development (no SSR), fails silently in production server build with a cryptic hydration mismatch or undefined context error.

antipattern.description: Path alias drift: tsconfig paths, vite.config resolve.alias, and components.json pointing to different locations.
antipattern.reason: Consumers get "cannot find module @/components/ui" in one build tool but not another; the root cause is hidden by different config files not staying in sync.

antipattern.description: Importing directly across feature boundaries (components/billing/ importing from components/dashboard/).
antipattern.reason: Cross-feature imports create invisible coupling; a change to one feature silently breaks another. Enforce with dependency-cruiser or eslint-plugin-boundaries.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Treat the architecture as a contract: (1) components/ui/* — vendored, only mutated via shadcn add; (2) components/<feature>/* — agents author here, importing from ui/ and lib/utils; (3) lib/ — pure utilities and tokens. Add CI guards: a "shadcn primitives untouched" check, a "no cross-feature imports" check, and a "no any types" check. For multi-tenant theming, scope variables under [data-theme=tenant-x] selectors and forbid hard-coded colors in features.

section.name: limitations
section.p: The "primitives are vendored" rule is hard to enforce socially; agents will reach into components/ui/ to "just fix one thing". Architecture rules without CI guards rot within weeks. Monorepo + shadcn CLI does not auto-detect package boundaries; agents need explicit --cwd flags. Themes via CSS variables work great until a feature needs a different theme (multi-tenant) — variable scoping has to be designed up front. Server vs client component boundaries leak through composition; lifting state up across the boundary often forces a rewrite.

section.name: cli-tools
section.p: shadcn CLI: Add primitives via add, diff via diff (npx shadcn@latest <cmd>). class-variance-authority: Variants in feature components (npm i class-variance-authority). tailwind-merge: cn() helper foundation (npm i tailwind-merge). madge: Detect cross-layer imports (npm i -D madge). dependency-cruiser: Enforce layering rules (npm i -D dependency-cruiser). eslint-plugin-boundaries: Lint layer imports (npm i -D eslint-plugin-boundaries). size-limit: Per-route bundle budget (npm i -D size-limit).

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Three layers, no more: ui/ (primitives), <feature>/ (compositions), layout/ (page chrome). Resist a fourth. All variants use cva(); banned: long inline class strings, dynamic class interpolation. One components.json per app or package; in monorepos pin per-package --cwd. Theme via CSS variables only — --background, --primary, --ring, etc. Components consume bg-background etc. For multi-tenant: scope variables under [data-theme="tenant-x"] and set on <html>. Server Components by default in Next.js App Router; mark client primitives explicitly with "use client". Treat upgrades as PRs: npx shadcn diff before bumping; visual regression must pass.

template.purpose: Import layer guard: primitives must not import features; no cross-feature imports.
Canonical cn() helper using clsx + tailwind-merge.
